seo.title: Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents in LangGraph Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix sensitive data leakage prevention in ai agents in LangGraph agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In LangGraph agents, sensitive data leakage occurs when PII, API keys, or confidential information from state, messages, or tools propagates through graph edges unchecked, especially in cyclical workflows or parallel branches. This manifests uniquely due to LangGraph's persistent checkpoints and stateful message passing, where leaked data can persist across invocations or leak via unfiltered tool outputs. Without proper sanitization at nodes or edges, agents inadvertently expose secrets in logs, responses, or external API calls.

howItManifests.scenario: User uploads document with PII to a document processing agent
howItManifests.symptom: Extracted names/SSNs appear unredacted in subsequent LLM tool calls and graph checkpoints
howItManifests.impact: PII exposed in vendor LLM logs and persistent checkpoint storage

howItManifests.scenario: Agent fetches internal API data using stored credentials
howItManifests.symptom: API keys visible in StateGraph message history and debug logs
howItManifests.impact: Secrets compromised if logs are shared or checkpoints persisted to cloud storage

howItManifests.scenario: Parallel research branches merge customer data from different sources
howItManifests.symptom: Unfiltered customer emails/phone numbers propagate to final response node
howItManifests.impact: Customer data leaked in agent final output to end user

howItManifests.scenario: Cyclical agent loops while processing sensitive support tickets
howItManifests.symptom: Ticket content with health/financial data logged repeatedly in infinite loop traces
howItManifests.impact: Massive data exposure in monitoring systems and debug outputs

detection.name: State Schema PII Scanner

detection.name: Runtime Message Leak Detector
detection.description: Intercepts graph state transitions to detect PII patterns in messages and tool outputs

detection.description: Scans persisted LangGraph checkpoints for sensitive data exposure

fixes.name: PII Sanitization State Node
fixes.description: Adds automatic PII redaction as a required graph node before tool calls and persistence

fixes.name: Secure State Schema with Redaction
fixes.description: Uses typed state with built-in redaction for sensitive fields

fixes.name: Vex-Verified Graph Invocation
fixes.description: Wraps all graph invokes with Vex verification for zero-trust leakage prevention

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides zero-config detection of sensitive data leakage in LangGraph agents by automatically wrapping graph.invoke() calls and scanning state transitions, checkpoints, and tool outputs for 50+ PII patterns including emails, API keys, SSNs, and custom secrets. Unlike manual regex scanning, Vex uses ML-powered context-aware detection that understands LangGraph's stateful nature and identifies leaks even in cyclical workflows or parallel branches.

Vex integrates as a single decorator or graph node, automatically redacting detected PII before it reaches LLMs/tools/persistence while preserving workflow functionality. For LangGraph specifically, Vex hooks into checkpoint serialization and state reducers to ensure no sensitive data ever persists, with full audit trails and auto-generated remediation code. Teams using Vex report 98% reduction in PII incidents with zero code changes to existing graphs.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex with `pip install vex-sdk` and protect your LangGraph agents from data leaks in 2 lines of code.
